Container.Name: emby-watchparty
Container.Overview: A synchronized watch party application for Emby media servers. Vue 3 SPA + FastAPI backend with per-user HLS transcodes, late-joiner voting, in-party admin panel, and drift correction. All streaming is proxied through the container so your Emby server can stay on your internal network. Only boot-essential values are set here; everything else (logging, security, rate limits, late-join vote, static session, force-transcode, etc.) is managed live via the /admin panel and persisted to config.json.
Container.Requires: A running Emby server reachable from this container and an admin-level Emby API key (Emby Dashboard > Advanced > API Keys). Per-user authentication is an in-app action -- any party member clicks "Login to Become Host" and supplies their own Emby credentials, so no long-lived user creds are stored on disk. Emby Premiere is required on the Emby side for hardware-accelerated transcoding (QSV, NVENC, VAAPI).

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the emby-watchparty web interface. Must match WATCH_PARTY_PORT below.

Config.Name: Emby Server URL
Config.Description: Full URL to your Emby server, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8096. Reachable from inside this container.

Config.Name: Emby API Key
Config.Description: Admin Emby API key. Generate one in Emby Dashboard > Advanced > API Keys. Per-user credentials are NOT set here -- users log in from inside the party UI.

Config.Name: Session Secret
Config.Description: Signing key for the party-bound session cookie. Generate ONCE with `openssl rand -hex 32` and leave it -- must persist across restarts and across every uvicorn worker. If left at the default, an ephemeral key is generated at boot with a loud warning; fine for local dev, catastrophic in production (every restart kicks everyone out).

Config.Name: Session Cookie Secure
Config.Description: When `true`, the session cookie carries the Secure flag so browsers only send it over HTTPS. Set `true` for every deployment behind TLS; leave `false` for local http://localhost dev (setting it true on an http origin locks users out).

Config.Name: Bind Address
Config.Description: Interface the app listens on inside the container. 0.0.0.0 listens on all interfaces (typical for Docker); rarely needs changing.

Config.Name: Container Port
Config.Description: Port the app binds inside the container. Must match the container-side of the Web UI Port mapping above.

Config.Name: App Prefix
Config.Description: URL prefix for reverse-proxy deployments behind a subpath (e.g. /watchparty). Leave empty for root-of-domain deployments. Backend routers, Socket.IO, /assets, and the SPA all honour the prefix at runtime -- no rebuild required.

Config.Name: CORS Allowed Origins
Config.Description: Comma-separated origin allowlist for the Socket.IO server. `*` accepts XHR-poll handshakes from any origin -- convenient for testing but pair with per-IP throttling. Pin to your real origin(s) for a proper deployment, e.g. `https://watchparty.example.com,https://staging.example.com`.

Config.Name: Session Expiry (seconds)
Config.Description: Session cookie lifetime in seconds. Default 86400 (24 hours).

Config.Name: Avatar Database
Config.Description: Persists the avatars.db SQLite database. Without this mount, every container recreate wipes every member's chosen avatar.

Config.Name: Avatar Images
Config.Description: Persists uploaded avatar image files. Paired with the Avatar Database mount above.

Config.Name: Admin Config (config.json)
Config.Description: Persists the runtime admin config (edited via /admin) across container recreates. IMPORTANT: `touch config.json` on the host BEFORE the first start, otherwise Docker silently creates it as an empty DIRECTORY and the backend fails to write.

Config.Name: Logs (optional)
Config.Description: Optional: persist application logs to disk. Only writes when Log To File is enabled in the /admin panel (default: console-only).
